World model: Clicking on a green object at (19,19) caused a board change. The green object seems to have changed color or shape. Need to compare before/after to understand the effect.
Plan: Compare the transition to see what changed, then determine the goal and optimal strategy.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after the mouse click
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}

# Check what changed - look for color changes
print("Before click - green nodes:")
for n in before['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, center~({sum(rows)//len(rows)},{sum(cols)//len(cols)})")

print("\nAfter click - all non-background nodes:")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('S', 'W'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({sum(rows)//len(rows)},{sum(cols)//len(cols)})")

# Check the region that was clicked
ascii_before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
ascii_after = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRegion around click (rows 16-22, cols 15-25):")
print("BEFORE:")
for r in range(16, 23):
    print(f"  {ascii_before[r][15:25]}")
print("AFTER:")
for r in range(16, 23):
    print(f"  {ascii_after[r][15:25]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Interesting! Clicking on the green object at (19,19) caused it to expand - it now has a gray (G) border around it. The green cross shape is still there but it's now surrounded by a gray frame. The node id=13 is the gray border (20 pixels, color G) and id=15 is the remaining green part (12 pixels, color N).
Also I notice some small gray (g) 2-pixel objects appeared nearby - these might be artifacts or part of some interaction pattern.
Let me think about what the goal could be:
1. Maybe I need to click all green objects to "activate" them
2. Maybe the gray border is a selection indicator
3. Maybe I need to move something
Let me try clicking on another green object to see if the same thing happens, or if the first one gets deselected.
